case_title: MARK HARPER VS. WELLS FARGO BANK ET AL

docket_entries.description: REQUEST TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HARPER, MARK ORDER FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS GRANTED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68634 (E), CRC 3.52
OPPOSITION TO AND PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANTS HEARING AND DEMURRERS_1157833 (TRANSACTION ID # 100016028) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HARPER, MARK
PROOF OF SERVICE OF OPPOSITION TO AND PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANTS HEARING AND DEMURRERS_1157833 (TRANSACTION ID # 100016028) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HARPER, MARK

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT WILL BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501, THE REAL PROPERTY AND HOUSING DEPARTMENT, ON APRIL 4, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M. FROM NOW ON ALL MOTIONS IN THIS CASE MUST BE NOTICED IN DEPARTMENT 501 AND COURTESY COPIES OF MOTION PAPERS DELIVERED TO THAT DEPARTMENT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK) **** NOT DISPO'D AS CONTINUANCE AT REQUEST OF DEPT. 501 AS MATTER ALREADY PREVIOUSLY ADDED BY DEPT. 501 ****

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT IS TIME-BARRED. THE THREE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR FRAUD-BASED CLAIMS APPLIES. PLAINTIFF OBTAINED HIS LOAN IN JULY 2008 AND DID NOT SUE UNTIL JULY 2015. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION DOES NOT CLAIM DELAYED DISCOVERY OF FRAUD. THE DEMURRER'S OTHER GROUNDS ARE MOOT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Continued To Jul-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Wells Fargo'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Wells Fargo's Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, because the complaint is time-barred. The three-year statute of limitations for fraud-based claims applies. Plaintiff obtained his loan in July 2008 and did not sue until July 2015. Plaintiff's opposition does not claim delayed discovery of fraud. The demurrer's other grounds are MOOT. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT will be heard in department 501, the real property and housing department, on April 4, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. From now on all motions in this case must be noticed in department 501 and courtesy copies of motion papers delivered to that department. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
